short_name: Inhaled Nitric Oxide for Microvascular Dysfunction in Traumatic Brain Injury
title: Inhaled Nitric Oxide for Microvascular Dysfunction in Traumatic Brain Injury

skipped_criteria: GCS 13-15 with an abnormal imaging scan: the conditional imaging requirement cannot be expressed with available fields and operators.
Radiologic findings indicative of primarily diffuse TBI: requires imaging interpretation not available in the schema.
Severe cardiac dysfunction (e.g. elevation of pulmonary edema on chest xray, large elevation of cardiac enzymes): requires cardiac/lab fields not in the schema.
Large focal injury (subdural hematoma, epidural hematoma, intraparenchymal hematoma, >30mL aggregate volume): requires imaging volume measurement not in the schema.
Need for immediate neurosurgical intervention: requires clinical decision field not in the schema.

Pre-existing disabling psychiatric or neurological disorders such as cortical stroke, brain tumor, disabling multiple sclerosis, dementia, and severe TBI: requires prior medical history fields not in the schema.
Known intracranial vessel disease: requires prior medical history fields not in the schema.
Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome (ARDS) or pre-existing pulmonary hypertension: requires respiratory/cardiac diagnosis fields not in the schema.
Cardiopulmonary resuscitation or cardioversion at admission: requires resuscitation status field not in the schema.
Chronic Kidney Disease (GFR <60mL/min/1.73m2): requires lab/renal function fields not in the schema.
Respiratory Infection: requires clinical diagnosis fields not in the schema.
Prisoners or patients in police custody: requires custody status field not in the schema.
Possible drug interactions (nitric oxide donors: prilocaine, sodium nitroprusside, nitroglycerin): requires current medication fields not in the schema.
